title: Eduversal Principal 360° Feedback Framework v1.0
description: Termly anonymous 360° feedback collection from staff, parent komite representatives, and student council representatives, structured around the 8 foci of principal-observation-rubric.json (P1-P8). Aggregate scores feed Principal Appraisal v1.0 framework F3 (weight 0.15). Charter NN5-compliant: aggregate scores only ever shared as a triplet with yayasan, never individual responses.
design_principles: Anonymity is non-negotiable. Below-threshold cohorts (< 5 respondents) are reported as 'insufficient response' — never with redacted individual data.
Same 8 foci as principal-observation-rubric.json. The principal sees one consistent leadership framework across observation, 360, and appraisal — not three competing models.

Audience-tailored questions per cohort. Staff see 'principal as supervisor'; parents see 'principal as community-leader'; students see 'principal as visible school leader'. The 8 foci are the same; the questions differ.
Termly cycles (W17, W38), not annual. Frequency builds trust + tracking.
Formative-first. Mid-year results (W17) are coaching input, not appraisal evidence. Year-end results (W38) are F3 evidence.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikbud 75/2016 — Komite Sekolah
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Parent komite is the formal channel for parent feedback per Indonesian regulation.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendiknas 13/2007 — Standar Kepala Sekolah
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Kompetensi sosial dimension — how the principal is perceived by the school community is part of the regulatory standard.

regulatory_alignment.title: PP 17/2025 — Perlindungan Anak di Ranah Digital
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Student responses must be collected with appropriate child-protection safeguards.

framework_anchors.principal_observation_rubric: principal-observation-rubric.json — same 8 foci P1-P8 used as 360 question categories
framework_anchors.principal_appraisal_v1: principal-appraisal-framework-v1.json F3 — this collection is the data_source for F3

cohort_definitions.label: Teaching Staff
cohort_definitions.anonymisation_rule: Always anonymous. Even with 5+ respondents, no individual response identifiable.
cohort_definitions.respondent_eligibility: All teaching staff at the school, regardless of length of service. Sub-leaders excluded from rating their own peer principal.
cohort_definitions.collection_method: Anonymous form distributed via TH; submission tracked by hash, not user id

cohort_definitions.label: Parent Komite Representatives
cohort_definitions.respondent_eligibility: Parent komite members + extended representative parents (3 per grade, randomly selected).
cohort_definitions.collection_method: Form sent via school's parent communication channel; principal does NOT see distribution list

cohort_definitions.label: Student Council Representatives
cohort_definitions.respondent_eligibility: Student council members (typically Grade 10-12 representatives). Younger students excluded — questions framed for age-appropriate response.
cohort_definitions.collection_method: In-school structured session, facilitated by Academic Coordinator (NOT principal). Confidentiality emphasised.

mid_year_cycle.purpose: FORMATIVE — coaching input only. Results shared with principal + Director-mentor only. Not entered into appraisal F3 data.

year_end_cycle.purpose: SUMMATIVE — feeds into Principal Appraisal F3. Aggregate triplet shared with yayasan per Charter NN5.

question_bank.common_instructions_to_respondents: These questions ask about the school principal's leadership over the academic year. Your responses are anonymous and aggregated with others. Aggregate results contribute to the principal's annual development planning. Please answer based on what you have observed; if you have not observed something, choose 'Cannot Comment'.

staff.text: The principal communicates a clear vision for our school's improvement that I understand and can describe.
Decisions about resources and programmes at our school visibly connect to a strategic plan.
I see evidence that our school is making progress against its stated improvement goals this year.

parent.text: I understand what the school is trying to achieve this year and how it plans to get there.
I see the school making improvements that match what the principal has communicated.

student.text: I know what our school is trying to be good at this year.
The principal has explained the school's plan to us in a way I understood.

staff.text: The principal is regularly visible in classrooms and engages with what is being taught.
The principal speaks knowledgeably about the curriculum we are teaching.
The principal initiates conversations with me about teaching and learning, not just operations.

parent.text: The principal can explain to parents what their children are learning and why it matters.

student.text: The principal seems interested in what we are learning in class — not just whether we are behaving well.

staff.text: Day-to-day operations at our school run smoothly under this principal's leadership.
When I raise an operational issue, it is addressed in a reasonable timeframe.
Communication from the principal's office is clear, timely, and respectful of my time.

parent.text: School communication to parents is timely, clear, and useful.
When I have asked the school office about something, I have received a satisfactory response.

student.text: Our school's day-to-day life feels organised — schedules, events, routines run on time.

staff.text: When the school identifies a problem (e.g., a subject's results), the principal acts on it visibly with data.
I have seen the principal use data to make decisions, not just to report them.

parent.text: The school shares meaningful information with me about my child's progress and the school's overall performance.

staff.text: The principal supports my professional development with relevant resources, coaching, or PD opportunities.
The principal recognises good teaching when it happens and engages with development areas constructively.
The principal cares about staff well-being in observable ways, not just in policy statements.
The performance-management process at our school is fair and developmental, not punitive.
The principal represents our school well to external stakeholders (yayasan, dinas, partners).

parent.text: I feel welcome at the school and treated as a partner in my child's education.
The principal is approachable when I have a concern or question.
The school engages parents meaningfully in school events, decisions, and improvement initiatives.

student.text: The principal listens to students and considers our perspectives.
I would feel comfortable raising a concern with the principal directly.

staff.text: I trust this school's safeguarding and child-protection procedures.
If I had a safeguarding concern, I would know how to raise it and trust it would be handled appropriately.

parent.text: I trust the school to keep my child safe — physically, emotionally, and digitally.

student.text: I feel safe and respected at school.
If something or someone made me feel unsafe at school, I know how to report it.

staff.text: The principal models the values and conduct that the school espouses to students and parents.
The principal makes decisions transparently and explains the reasoning behind them.
The principal handles disagreement and feedback professionally.

parent.text: The principal embodies the values and ethics I want my child's school to stand for.

student.text: The principal is fair when dealing with students.
The principal treats students with respect.

narrative_fields.text: What is one thing the principal does well that should continue?
What is one thing the principal could do differently to lead our school better?
Optional — anything else you want the Eduversal Academic Director to know about leadership at our school this year?

data_aggregation_rules.scoring: For each focus (P1-P8), compute weighted aggregate from cohorts (staff 0.6 / parent 0.25 / student 0.15). Within cohort, average across questions. Exclude 'Cannot Comment' (value 0). Multiply by 25 to convert to 100-scale.
data_aggregation_rules.cohort_weighting_when_below_threshold: If a cohort has < 5 respondents, redistribute its weight proportionally to the remaining cohorts. If only one cohort meets threshold, use only that cohort's data with a flag in the report.
data_aggregation_rules.narrative_aggregation: Free-text responses are reviewed by the Director (mentor) and a thematic summary is included in the appraisal report. Individual quotes are NEVER attributed.

reporting.to_principal_at_w17: Per-focus aggregate (formative). Themes from narrative responses. Cohort breakdown.
reporting.to_principal_at_w38: Same as W17 + comparison vs. mid-year + appraisal F3 contribution preview.
reporting.to_yayasan_at_w39: Aggregate F3 score only as part of NN5 triplet. NEVER per-focus, NEVER per-question, NEVER per-cohort.

charter_compliance.NN1: Mid-year cycle (W17) is formative-only — does NOT enter appraisal data. Year-end cycle (W38) feeds F3 — that is the SUMMATIVE moment, NOT during the year.
charter_compliance.NN2: The principal's own induction journal is not part of this 360. NN2 protects journal; this is a separate stakeholder feedback instrument.
charter_compliance.NN5: Yayasan-facing report is the appraisal triplet only. Per-focus 360 scores never leave Eduversal HQ + principal.

cycle_doc_fields.cohort_response_counts: map (staff: N, parent: N, student: N)

response_doc_fields.respondentHashId: string (one-way hash of user id + cycleId, NOT user id)

access_control.principal: read aggregate cycle doc only (never response docs); narrative themes summary only
access_control.academic_director_hq: read aggregate cycle doc + narrative themes + narrativeQ3_internal; never response docs by user-mapping
access_control.central_admin: read aggregate + responses (for system maintenance only); audit-logged

phase_2_implementation.firestore_rules_to_add: principal_360_cycles + responses subcoll — write-once-by-respondent, aggregate maintained by Cloud Function on each new response
phase_2_implementation.cloud_function_needed: onPrincipal360ResponseWritten — recompute aggregate on each submission
phase_2_implementation.estimated_effort: 8 weeks of in-house dev (3 pages + rules + Cloud Function + testing)

provenance.regulatory_sources: Permendikbud 75/2016 (komite), Permendiknas 13/2007 (kompetensi sosial), PP 17/2025 (digital child protection)
provenance.audited: 2026-05-08 cross-module Round 2 — closes the gap identified where principal_appraisal_v1 F3 data_source had no concrete spec
